channel.title: Package-Management on OpenSwap Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Package-Management on OpenSwap Guide

item.title: pypiserver vs devpi vs Bandersnatch: Self-Hosted PyPI Mirror Guide 2026
item.description: If your team depends on Python packages from PyPI, running a self-hosted mirror gives you faster installs, offline resilience, and full control over which versions enter your environment. Whether you need a lightweight private registry for internal wheels, a caching proxy to speed up CI pipelines, or a full PyPI mirror for air-gapped networks, the right tool makes a big difference.

item.title: Self-Hosted Package Registry: Nexus vs Verdaccio vs Pulp Guide 2026
item.description:
Why Self-Host Your Package Registry?

Every modern software project depends on external packages — npm modules, Python wheels, Maven artifacts, Docker images. Pulling these directly from public registries introduces real risks and costs that compound as your infrastructure grows.
